meta.title: Linguist - Translate Web Pages Privately | Browser Translator Extension for Chrome & Firefox
meta.description: Privacy‑first browser translator extension for Chrome and Firefox. Translate web pages, selected text, subtitles and messages instantly. Works offline and supports translation service integrations and APIs.

hero.title: Translate web pages fluently
hero.description:
Linguist is a flexible browser translator powered by the most popular translation services.

Translate entire web pages, selected text, subtitles on Netflix or in video calls, and private messages. Listen to translations and save important ones to your personal dictionary to learn new words, even offline.

Linguist is a modern, privacy‑first alternative to Google Translate browser extensions. Try it for free.

opensource.title: Open source
opensource.content:
Linguist is a free and <0>open‑source</0> browser translation extension. It does not collect or sell user data.

You can <1>donate</1> to support Linguist and help us maintain independence and high quality.

Share Linguist on social media to help others discover it.

support.title: Support
support.content:
If you have questions, contact us <0/>.
If you found a bug and have a GitHub account, please <1>create an issue</1>.

offlineTranslation.title: Offline translation and privacy
offlineTranslation.content:
Translate text directly on your device with the built‑in offline translator. Your text stays private and is not sent over the internet.

Enable this feature in the options page to keep translations private when working with emails, documents, or personal messages.

The offline translator also allows you to translate pages without an internet connection, which is especially useful while traveling.

fullPageTranslation.title: Translate entire web pages
fullPageTranslation.content:
Fast, high‑quality full‑page translation in just a few clicks.

Use a hotkey to translate instantly. Hover over translated text to see the original. Translate even private pages behind login forms or paywalls.

Set up automatic translation rules based on page language or domain.

Linguist follows a minimal page‑modification approach: it changes only text nodes and preserves the page layout and user experience.

Enable real‑time translation for cross‑language meetings on Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams.

selectedTextTranslation.title: Translate selected text on any website
selectedTextTranslation.content:
Encounter unfamiliar words while reading an online article?
Simply select text on the page and click the translate button.

Listen to both the original and translated text, and save translations to your dictionary to review later.

textTranslation.title: Quick text translation
textTranslation.content:
Need to translate something quickly? Click the Linguist icon and enter your text.

No need to switch between tabs or open translation websites. Translate directly in your browser using the service you prefer.

knowledgeBase.title: Build your personal knowledge base
knowledgeBase.content:
Every translation is saved in your history, and you can add important entries to your personal dictionary. Search your dictionary and translation history anytime.

Nothing is lost, even during long research or learning sessions.

The dictionary works offline, making Linguist ideal for language learners and travelers who need constant access to their word lists.

customTranslators.title: Integrate Translation Services and APIs
customTranslators.content:
Linguist lets you choose which translation service to use.

It supports popular translation providers such as Google, Yandex, and Microsoft, with more than 100 languages available.

If a service you need is not included, you can add it as a <0>custom translator</0>.

Custom translators allow you to integrate any translation system — from modern LLM services such as ChatGPT, DeepL, DeepSeek, and Gemini to local translators running on your device, such as Ollama or LibreTranslate.

whatIsLinguist.title: What is Linguist?
whatIsLinguist.content:
Linguist is a privacy‑first browser translation extension for Chrome and Firefox.

It lets you translate full web pages, selected text, subtitles, and messages directly in your browser without switching to external translation websites.

whyLinguist.title: Why use Linguist?
whyLinguist.content:
There are many browser extensions that translate web pages, and some browsers even include built‑in translators. So why use Linguist?

Most built‑in translators and translation extensions are limited in flexibility. Many rely on a single translation provider such as Google Translate or Microsoft Bing, while Firefox offers its own offline translator. In most cases, you cannot choose the service that best fits your needs.

If the translation quality is not good enough for your use case, or if sending private text to third‑party services is not acceptable, many tools leave you without a good alternative.

Many tools also focus on only one task. Some translate full pages, while others translate only selected text.

At the same time, some translation extensions are built primarily around monetization. Features such as translation history or personal dictionaries may require a subscription, and sometimes even basic features like a dark theme are treated as premium options.

Linguist takes a different approach. It is designed as a practical translation tool. You can choose the translation service that works best for you, switch between providers, or add your own custom translators.

Linguist also brings everything together in one place: translate entire web pages, selected text, subtitles, and messages while keeping track of important translations in your history and personal dictionary.

theLinguistLogo.title: Why is the Linguist logo the Tower of Babel?
theLinguistLogo.content:
The Linguist logo is inspired by the <wiki>Tower of Babel</wiki>, one of the oldest stories about language and communication.

According to the story, people once spoke the same language and tried to build a tower reaching the sky. The tower was never completed, and humanity was scattered across many languages. Since then, language differences have often made communication more difficult.

Linguist was created to help solve this problem.

The Tower of Babel in the logo is a reminder of that story: many languages, many cultures, and the constant human effort to understand one another. Linguist helps bridge those gaps by making translation simple, flexible, and accessible directly in your browser.

The logo also reflects the spirit of the project. Linguist is not just another wrapper around "Google Translate". It is built by people who care about languages, privacy, and open technology. The Tower of Babel represents curiosity, learning, and the idea that language differences should bring people together rather than keep them apart.

imageTranslation.title: Why doesn’t Linguist translate text in images?
imageTranslation.content:
Linguist focuses on reliable and high‑quality translation features.

We have researched different approaches to translating text inside images. At the moment, most available solutions do not provide consistent results, especially when running directly in the browser — which is important for maintaining user privacy.

Because of this, Linguist does not currently include image translation. We prefer not to ship features that do not meet our quality standards.

If we find a reliable and privacy‑respecting way to implement image translation in the future, we would be happy to add it.

howTranslatePages.title: How does Linguist translate web pages?
howTranslatePages.content:
Linguist can translate entire web pages in just a few clicks. You can start page translation using the extension button or a keyboard shortcut.

The original text always remains accessible. Hover over translated text to see the original.

offlineTranslation.title: Can Linguist translate pages offline?
offlineTranslation.content:
Yes. Linguist includes a built‑in offline translator that allows you to translate text without sending data over the internet.

This helps protect your privacy and allows you to translate content even without an internet connection.

privacy.title: Is Linguist private?
privacy.content:
Yes. Linguist is designed with privacy in mind.

The extension does not collect or sell user data. When using the <offline-translator>offline translator</offline-translator>, translations are processed entirely on your device.

supportedBrowsers.title: Which browsers does Linguist support?
supportedBrowsers.content:
Linguist is available for Chrome and Firefox. It also works in other Chromium‑based browsers that support Chrome extensions.

Linguist also works in Android browsers that support extensions, such as Firefox for Android and some Chromium‑based browsers.

selectedText.title: Can Linguist translate selected text?
selectedText.content:
Yes. Simply select text on any web page and use the translate button to translate it instantly.

You can also listen to the translation and save it to your personal dictionary.

customTranslators.title: Can I use my own translation service?
customTranslators.content:
Yes. Linguist supports <custom-translator>custom translators</custom-translator>, which allow you to integrate additional translation services.

You can connect external APIs or local translation systems such as <libre-translate>LibreTranslate</libre-translate> or <ollama>Ollama</ollama>.

isFree.title: Is Linguist free?
isFree.content:
Yes. Linguist is completely free and <linguist>open source</linguist>.

The project is supported by the community, and you can optionally <donations>support it with a donation</donations>.
